questions.question: Define Agile Process and explain its key characteristics.
keyPoints: Agile is a modern software development methodology emphasizing flexibility
Iterative development with small incremental cycles
Collaboration between cross-functional teams
Rapid delivery of working software
Embraces changing requirements even late in development

questions.question: Explain the four values of the Agile Manifesto.
keyPoints: Individuals and interactions over processes and tools
Working software over comprehensive documentation
Customer collaboration over contract negotiation
Responding to change over following a plan

questions.question: Differentiate between Agile and Waterfall methodology.
keyPoints: Waterfall is sequential; Agile is iterative
Waterfall has rigid phases; Agile allows flexibility
Waterfall requires complete requirements upfront; Agile welcomes changes
Waterfall delivers at end; Agile delivers incrementally
Agile involves customer more frequently throughout development

questions.question: Explain the three roles in Scrum framework with their responsibilities.
keyPoints: Product Owner: Manages Product Backlog, prioritizes features, represents stakeholders
Scrum Master: Facilitates Scrum events, removes impediments, coaches the team
Development Team: Cross-functional, self-organizing, delivers product increments

questions.question: What is a Sprint in Agile? Describe its key components.
keyPoints: Time-boxed iteration (2-4 weeks)
Sprint Planning: defines what can be delivered
Daily Scrum: daily synchronization
Sprint Review: demonstrates increment to stakeholders
Sprint Retrospective: team reflects and improves
No changes mid-sprint; scope can beclarified

questions.question: Explain the concept of User Story in Agile with an example.
keyPoints: A short, simple description of a feature
Written from end-user perspective
Format: As a [user], I want [goal] so that [benefit]
Focuses on value delivered to user
Example: 'As a student, I want to download lecture notes so that I can study offline'

questions.question: List and explain any four principles of the Agile Manifesto.
keyPoints: Early and continuous delivery of valuable software
Welcome changing requirements
Deliver working software frequently
Business and developers must work together daily
Build projects around motivated individuals
Face-to-face conversation
Working software is primary measure of progress
Sustainable development
Continuous attention to technical excellence
Simplicity

questions.question: Explain Agile Manifesto in detail. Discuss its four values and twelve principles.
keyPoints: Agile Manifesto formulated in 2001 by 17 software developers

Four Values: (1) Individuals and interactions over processes and tools, (2) Working software over comprehensive documentation, (3) Customer collaboration over contract negotiation, (4) Responding to change over following a plan

Twelve Principles: Customer satisfaction through early delivery, Welcome changing requirements, Frequent delivery of working software, Business-developer collaboration, Motivated individuals, Face-to-face communication, Working software as progress measure, Sustainable development pace, Technical excellence and good design, Simplicity, Self-organizing teams, Regular reflection and adjustment

questions.question: Describe the Scrum framework in detail with reference to its roles, events, and artifacts.
keyPoints: Scrum is an Agile framework for developing complex products
Roles: Product Owner (backlog management), Scrum Master (facilitator), Development Team (3-9 members, self-organizing)
Events: Sprint (2-4 weeks), Sprint Planning, Daily Scrum (15 min), Sprint Review, Sprint Retrospective
Artifacts: Product Backlog (ordered list of requirements), Sprint Backlog (items selected for Sprint), Increment (sum of completed items)
Key characteristics: Time-boxed, empirical process control, transparency

questions.question: Compare Agile methodology with traditional Waterfall model. Explain when each is appropriate to use.
keyPoints: Waterfall: Sequential, rigid, requires complete requirements upfront, changes difficult, one delivery at end
Agile: Iterative, flexible, welcomes changes, multiple deliveries
When to use Waterfall: Fixed requirements, well-understood domain, regulatory compliance, short projects
When to use Agile: Uncertain requirements, need for quick feedback, complex projects, competitive markets
Agile provides better risk management through iterative delivery
Waterfall better for projects where changes are costly

questions.question: Explain Extreme Programming (XP) methodology. How does it differ from Scrum?
keyPoints: XP is an Agile methodology focusing on software quality and responsiveness to changing requirements
Key practices: Pair programming, Test-Driven Development (TDD), Continuous Integration, Refactoring, Simple design
User stories like Scrum
Planning game for release planning
Differences from Scrum: XP is engineering-focused, Scrum is management-focused
XP prescribes specific technical practices, Scrum doesn't
XP has continuous feedback loops, Scrum has sprint-based feedback
Scrum roles: Product Owner, Scrum Master, Team; XP roles less formalized
